flashcards.front: What is the primary reason for converting an NFA to a DFA?
flashcards.back: To make it more suitable for implementation in digital systems due to its deterministic nature.

flashcards.front: What is the other name for NFA to DFA conversion?
What is the main difference between NFA and DFA?
flashcards.back: NFA can be in multiple states simultaneously, while DFA can only be in one state at a time.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of subset construction?
flashcards.back: To convert an NFA into a DFA that recognizes the same language.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of subset construction?
flashcards.back: O(2^n), where n is the number of states in the NFA.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of subset construction?
flashcards.back: O(2^n), where n is the number of states in the NFA.

flashcards.front: What is the main application of NFA to DFA conversion?
flashcards.back: Compiler design and other applications where deterministic automata are required.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a deterministic and non-deterministic automaton?
flashcards.back: A deterministic automaton can only be in one state at a time, while a non-deterministic automaton can be in multiple states simultaneously.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the power set in subset construction?
flashcards.back: To represent all possible subsets of states in the NFA.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the transition function in subset construction?
flashcards.back: To define the next state for each subset of states in the NFA.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the initial state in subset construction?
flashcards.back: To define the starting state of the DFA.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the accepting states in subset construction?
flashcards.back: To define the states that accept the input string in the DFA.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a Moore machine and a Mealy machine?
flashcards.back: A Moore machine has the output associated with the state, while a Mealy machine has the output associated with the transition.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the equivalence relation in subset construction?
flashcards.back: To define the equivalence of states in the NFA.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the quotient set in subset construction?
flashcards.back: To define the set of equivalence classes of states in the NFA.
